overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a highly targeted and professional social media presence focused on computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated behavior. Across all platforms, his audience is highly specialized and deeply engaged with academic outputs, software tool releases, and career opportunities. While LinkedIn provides the highest absolute reach, Bluesky demonstrates the strongest community engagement relative to follower size, making it a highly effective space for research dissemination.

contentThemes.description: Research, publications, and conference presentations addressing online manipulation and coordinated link-sharing campaigns.
Software development updates, such as CooRnet v2.0, and methodological approaches to platform analysis.
Announcements for postdoctoral hiring, PhD opportunities, and prestigious research grant awards.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's high organic engagement rate to post more interactive content and call-to-actions, such as community Q&As about software tools.
Utilize LinkedIn's larger professional network more frequently for career-oriented announcements to maximize the applicant pool for postdoctoral positions.
Increase posting frequency on Mastodon by sharing bite-sized methodological insights to match the platform's tech-oriented and academic user base.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky is the most interactive platform with an average engagement rate of 6.14%, driven by strong community interest in new academic papers and student opportunities.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon provides a highly targeted niche audience, responding exceptionally well to open-source software and technical tool updates like CooRnet.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn delivers the highest absolute volume of engagement, making it highly effective for major professional updates such as grant awards and recruitment.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
